title: Red Hat Security Advisory: libsoup3 security update
overview:
An update for libsoup3 is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: libsoup3 security update

An update for libsoup3 is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for libsoup3 is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
libsoup3-3.6.5-3.el10_1.10.src.rpm
libsoup3-3.6.5-3.el10_1.10.x86_64.rpm
libsoup3-debuginfo-3.6.5-3.el10_1.10.x86_64.rpm
libsoup3-debugsource-3.6.5-3.el10_1.10.x86_64.rpm
libsoup3-devel-3.6.5-3.el10_1.10.x86_64.rpm
libsoup3-3.6.5-3.el10_1.10.s390x.rpm
libsoup3-debuginfo-3.6.5-3.el10_1.10.s390x.rpm
libsoup3-debugsource-3.6.5-3.el10_1.10.s390x.rpm
libsoup3-devel-3.6.5-3.el10_1.10.s390x.rpm
libsoup3-3.6.5-3.el10_1.10.ppc64le.rpm
libsoup3-debuginfo-3.6.5-3.el10_1.10.ppc64le.rpm
libsoup3-debugsource-3.6.5-3.el10_1.10.ppc64le.rpm
libsoup3-devel-3.6.5-3.el10_1.10.ppc64le.rpm
libsoup3-3.6.5-3.el10_1.10.aarch64.rpm
libsoup3-debuginfo-3.6.5-3.el10_1.10.aarch64.rpm
libsoup3-debugsource-3.6.5-3.el10_1.10.aarch64.rpm
libsoup3-devel-3.6.5-3.el10_1.10.aarch64.rpm
libsoup3-doc-3.6.5-3.el10_1.10.noarch.rpm

CVE-2026-1761: A flaw was found in libsoup. This stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability occurs during the parsing of multipart HTTP responses due to an incorrect length calculation. A remote attacker can exploit this by sending a specially crafted multipart HTTP response, which can lead to memory corruption. This issue may result in application crashes or arbitrary code execution in applications that process untrusted server responses, and it does not require authentication or user interaction.
